wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer project overview. Explain the system's purpose as an automated review analysis platform that collects Google Play Store reviews, applies multi-layered filtering, generates AI-powered themes, and produces weekly insights reports. Document the three-phase implementation approach, from basic scraping to advanced LLM integration. Include the technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, SQLite/PostgreSQL, Groq LLM, SMTP), core components, and end-to-end data flow. Provide both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'weekly pulse', 'theme assignment', and 'PII scrubbing'. Include diagrams where appropriate to illustrate the system architecture and data pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer system architecture. Explain the layered architecture design with presentation layer (Express API), business logic layer (service classes), data access layer (SQLite/PostgreSQL), and integration layer (external services). Document the three-phase implementation approach with separate codebases for scraping, analytics, and UI. Detail the component relationships, data flows between modules, and integration patterns with Google Play Store, Groq LLM, and SMTP providers. Include system context diagrams showing how the application interacts with external systems. Address cross-cutting concerns like logging, error handling, and security. Document the technology stack choices and architectural decisions that enable scalability and maintainability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Scraper Module
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core Google Play Store scraper module. Detail the token-based pagination strategy that enables systematic review collection with built-in safety limits of 50 pages. Explain the integration with google-play-scraper library, including API rate limiting, error handling, and retry mechanisms. Document the scraping workflow from initial app URL to processed review data. Include practical examples of scraping configurations, custom filters, and performance optimization techniques. Address common scraping challenges, IP blocking prevention, and data consistency validation. Provide troubleshooting guidance for network issues, API changes, and malformed responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Integration with Groq
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the LLM integration with Groq in Phase 2. Document the GroqClient implementation including API configuration, authentication setup, and request/response handling. Explain the prompt engineering strategies used for theme generation, including structured prompts, role definitions, and instruction formatting. Detail the JSON schema validation process for LLM responses, error handling mechanisms, and retry logic. Document the theme generation workflow from raw review text to validated theme objects with confidence scores. Include practical examples of prompt construction, response parsing, and error recovery scenarios. Address performance optimization techniques, rate limiting considerations, and cost management strategies for LLM usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 1 API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Phase 1 endpoints focused on review scraping and retrieval. Document the POST /api/reviews/scrape endpoint for initiating review collection with optional maxReviews parameter, including request/response schemas, error handling, and success responses. Detail the GET /api/reviews/scrape endpoint for browser-friendly triggering with query parameter support. Document the GET /api/reviews endpoint for retrieving stored reviews with pagination support via limit parameter. Include concrete examples of request/response payloads, HTTP status codes, parameter validation rules, and error scenarios. Provide client implementation guidelines for both POST and GET methods, addressing rate limiting considerations and best practices for review collection workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Include prerequisites (Node.js, npm, Git), environment setup instructions, dependency installation, and initial configuration. Document environment variable requirements for Google Play Scraper, Groq API, database connections, and SMTP configuration. Provide step-by-step setup for both phase-1 and phase-2 implementations. Include first-time usage examples for triggering scrapers, generating themes, and creating weekly pulses. Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Make content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase Implementation Plan
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the three-phase implementation plan of the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document Phase 1 (core scraping, filtering, and storage) with google-play-scraper integration, multi-layered filtering pipeline, and SQLite persistence. Explain Phase 2 (advanced analytics and automation) with Groq LLM integration, theme generation, weekly pulse creation, and email automation. Detail Phase 3 (web UI and email triggers) with frontend web application, user preference management, and automated scheduling. Include the rationale behind the phased approach, dependencies between phases, and how each phase builds upon previous implementations. Document the folder structure organization, shared architectural concepts across phases, and migration strategies between phases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Filtering Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the multi-layered filtering pipeline that ensures data quality and privacy. Document the PII detection system for identifying and removing emails, phone numbers, and other sensitive information. Explain duplicate detection algorithms using review content hashing and metadata comparison. Detail the emoji filtering mechanism and word count validation rules. Include the filtering chain workflow, performance considerations, and configurable thresholds. Provide examples of filter configurations, custom rule implementation, and debugging techniques. Address false positive handling, filter bypass scenarios, and quality metrics for filtered data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Generation & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the theme generation and management system. Explain the theme creation workflow from processed reviews to validated themes with confidence scoring. Document the theme validation rules, uniqueness criteria, and temporal validity constraints. Detail the theme persistence strategy, including database schema extensions and relationship mapping to reviews. Explain the theme categorization system, metadata handling, and search capabilities. Include examples of theme generation processes, validation failures, and manual intervention scenarios. Document the theme lifecycle management, archiving strategies, and cleanup procedures. Address performance considerations for bulk theme generation and concurrent processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 2 API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Phase 2 endpoints covering advanced analytics, automation, and user management. Document theme management endpoints including theme generation, validation, and retrieval operations. Detail pulse generation endpoints for weekly insights creation, theme assignment, and content aggregation. Document user preference management endpoints for scheduling configuration, delivery preferences, and subscription management. Include email service endpoints for testing SMTP configuration and delivery verification. Provide detailed request/response schemas, authentication requirements, parameter validation rules, and error handling patterns. Include concrete examples of complex workflows such as theme-to-pulse assignment, automated scheduling, and preference-based filtering. Address API versioning, rate limiting, and security considerations for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 1: Review Scraper
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Phase 1 of the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document the core review scraping functionality using google-play-scraper, including token-based pagination strategy with 50-page safety limits. Explain the multi-layered filtering pipeline covering PII detection (emails, phone numbers), duplicate removal, emoji filtering, and word count validation. Detail the SQLite database schema and data storage patterns. Document the review service business logic and REST API endpoints for scraping and retrieving reviews. Include practical examples of scraping workflows, filter configurations, and database operations. Address error handling, performance considerations, and integration patterns with the broader system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack used in the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document the primary technologies including TypeScript/JavaScript for backend development, Node.js runtime environment, and Express framework for API development. Detail the database technologies with SQLite for local development and PostgreSQL for production environments. Explain the external service integrations including google-play-scraper for Play Store review extraction, Groq LLM for AI-powered theme generation, and SMTP providers for email delivery. Document the development tools, build system, testing frameworks, and deployment technologies. Include version compatibility requirements, dependency management strategies, and technology selection rationale for each component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SQLite database layer and data persistence architecture. Detail the database schema design for the Review entity, including field definitions, data types, and constraints. Document the connection management, transaction handling, and performance optimization strategies. Explain the repository pattern implementation for data access operations. Include database initialization scripts, migration strategies, and backup procedures. Provide examples of CRUD operations, query optimization, and indexing strategies. Address concurrency handling, data integrity constraints, and troubleshooting common database issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review-to-Theme Assignment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the review-to-theme assignment system. Document the batch processing strategy for assigning themes to reviews, including algorithm selection and confidence thresholding. Explain the assignment workflow from theme availability to review tagging, including conflict resolution and manual override capabilities. Detail the performance optimization techniques for bulk assignment operations, including transaction management and indexing strategies. Document the assignment validation process, duplicate handling, and assignment history tracking. Include examples of assignment scenarios, edge cases, and troubleshooting common issues. Address scalability considerations for large-scale review datasets and concurrent assignment operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phase 2: Advanced Analytics & Automation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Phase 2 of the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document the advanced LLM integration with Groq for theme generation, review-to-theme assignment, and weekly pulse creation. Explain the AI-powered analytics workflow including prompt engineering, JSON schema validation, and confidence scoring. Detail the email service implementation with SMTP configuration and template management. Document the automated job scheduling system for weekly pulse generation and delivery. Cover user preferences management, database schema extensions, and API endpoints for theme management, pulse generation, and user preferences. Include practical examples of LLM interactions, scheduling workflows, and email automation. Address performance optimization, error handling, and monitoring strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: End-to-End Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the end-to-end data flow of the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document the complete pipeline from Google Play Store review scraping through filtering, storage, AI-powered theme generation, and weekly pulse creation. Explain each stage including token-based pagination with 50-page safety limits, multi-criteria filtering (PII removal, duplicates, quality checks), SQLite database storage with normalized schemas, Groq LLM integration for theme discovery and assignment, and weekly pulse generation with action recommendations. Detail the data transformations at each stage, error handling mechanisms, and data validation processes. Include diagrams illustrating the flow between components and highlight critical data points like PII scrubbing and content sanitization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the review service business logic layer. Document the service methods for collecting, filtering, and storing reviews from the scraper pipeline. Explain the data transformation processes, validation rules, and error handling strategies. Detail the integration with the database layer and API endpoints. Include examples of service method usage, batch processing workflows, and performance optimization techniques. Address concurrent access patterns, transaction boundaries, and data consistency guarantees. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common service errors and integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Weekly Pulse Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the weekly pulse generation system. Document the pulse creation workflow from assigned themes to aggregated insights with action recommendations. Explain the pulse aggregation algorithms, sentiment analysis integration, and trend identification processes. Detail the pulse content formatting, including HTML template rendering and plain text alternatives. Document the pulse validation and quality assurance processes, including completeness checks and recommendation scoring. Include examples of pulse generation scenarios, template customization, and content personalization. Address performance optimization for weekly batch processing, memory management, and error recovery strategies. Document the pulse distribution mechanism and delivery tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer REST endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for both phase-1 and phase-2 APIs. Include detailed specifications for review scraping endpoints, data retrieval endpoints, theme management endpoints, pulse generation endpoints, and user preference management. Provide concrete examples of request/response payloads, error codes, and status messages. Document parameter validation, rate limiting, and security considerations. Include client implementation guidelines, common usage patterns, and integration examples. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and deprecation policies where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Phase 1 REST endpoints. Document the HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and error handling for review collection and retrieval. Detail the /api/reviews/scrape endpoint for triggering manual scraping and the /api/reviews endpoint for data retrieval. Include authentication methods, rate limiting, and request validation. Provide practical examples of API usage, response formats, and error codes. Document common use cases, client implementation guidelines, and integration patterns. Address CORS configuration, content-type handling, and API versioning considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Service & Delivery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the email service and automated delivery system. Document the SMTP configuration process, including provider setup, authentication methods, and security considerations. Explain the email template management system, including HTML/CSS templating, dynamic content injection, and responsive design considerations. Detail the email queue management, delivery tracking, and bounce handling mechanisms. Document the user preferences integration for personalized email delivery, including subscription management and preference validation. Include examples of email composition, template customization, and delivery scenarios. Address deliverability best practices, spam prevention measures, and email analytics tracking. Document error handling for failed deliveries and retry mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for configuration management in the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document all environment variables including Google Play Scraper credentials, Groq API keys, database connection strings, SMTP configuration, and application-specific settings. Explain configuration loading patterns, validation rules, and default value handling. Detail database configuration for both SQLite (development) and PostgreSQL (production) environments. Document security best practices for credential management, environment variable protection, and configuration drift prevention. Include examples of configuration files, deployment-specific settings, and troubleshooting common configuration issues. Address configuration hot-reloading, validation strategies, and monitoring configuration changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Automated Job Scheduling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the automated job scheduling system. Document the cron-based scheduling configuration, including weekly triggers, timezone handling, and execution windows. Explain the job orchestration workflow from schedule trigger to completion, including dependency management and parallel processing. Detail the error handling and recovery mechanisms, including job failure notifications and retry strategies. Document the monitoring and logging systems for scheduled job execution, including metrics collection and alerting. Include examples of scheduling configurations, job execution scenarios, and troubleshooting failed jobs. Address performance considerations for long-running jobs, resource management, and system load balancing. Document the job status tracking and historical execution reporting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer database schema. Detail entity relationships between reviews, themes, review_themes, weekly_pulses, user_preferences, and scheduled_jobs tables. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints for each table. Explain data validation rules, business logic constraints, and referential integrity requirements. Include database schema diagrams and sample data examples. Document data access patterns, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle, retention policies, and archival rules. Include data migration paths, version management, and schema evolution strategies. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Preferences Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the user preferences management system. Document the preference storage schema, including subscription types, delivery frequencies, and content preferences. Explain the preference validation rules, update mechanisms, and change tracking. Detail the integration with email service for personalized delivery based on user preferences. Document the preference API endpoints, including CRUD operations and validation responses. Include examples of preference configurations, subscription scenarios, and preference updates. Address privacy considerations for preference storage, data protection measures, and user consent management. Document the preference migration strategies and backward compatibility handling for preference schema changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document unit testing approaches for individual components including filter functions, service layers, and utility modules. Explain integration testing strategies for end-to-end workflows covering scraping, filtering, LLM interactions, and email delivery. Detail test data management, mock strategies for external services, and test environment setup. Include testing best practices for asynchronous operations, error scenarios, and edge cases. Document continuous integration patterns, test coverage requirements, and quality gates. Address performance testing, load testing considerations, and monitoring test execution. Provide examples of test implementations and debugging strategies for test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document environment setup for development, staging, and production deployments. Explain containerization strategies, Docker configuration, and orchestration requirements. Detail monitoring and logging setup including centralized logging, performance metrics, and alerting strategies. Document backup and recovery procedures, disaster recovery planning, and maintenance schedules. Include scaling considerations, load balancing, and high availability configurations. Address security operations, vulnerability management, and compliance requirements. Document operational procedures for troubleshooting, incident response, and system maintenance. Provide runbooks for common operational tasks and escalation procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Privacy & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive privacy and security documentation for the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document PII protection measures including automatic detection and removal of sensitive data during scraping, filtering, and LLM processing. Explain data sanitization processes, encryption strategies, and secure data transmission. Detail security best practices for API endpoints, database access, and external service integrations. Document compliance considerations for data protection regulations and industry standards. Include security audit procedures, vulnerability assessment strategies, and incident response protocols. Address access control mechanisms, authentication requirements, and authorization patterns. Document secure coding practices, input validation, and protection against common security threats. Provide guidelines for secure deployment and operational security measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for contributing to the Groww App Review Insights Analyzer. Document code standards and conventions for TypeScript development, including naming patterns, file organization, and architectural principles. Explain the contribution workflow including branch strategies, pull request procedures, and code review processes. Detail development environment setup, debugging techniques, and local testing procedures. Document release procedures, versioning strategies, and deployment pipelines. Include guidelines for documentation standards, commit message conventions, and issue reporting. Address code quality tools, linting rules, and automated quality checks. Provide examples of good coding practices and common anti-patterns to avoid. Document team collaboration tools, communication protocols, and project governance.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Groww App Review Insights Analyzer - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

The Groww App Review Insights Analyzer is a sophisticated multi-phase system designed to automatically collect, analyze, and synthesize user feedback from the Groww Android app's Google Play Store reviews. The project follows a progressive development approach across three distinct phases, transforming from a simple review scraper to a comprehensive insights platform with automated weekly pulse generation and email delivery.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Automated Review Collection**: Scrape public Google Play Store reviews for the Groww app (`com.nextbillion.groww`)
- **Intelligent Analysis**: Extract meaningful themes and insights from user feedback
- **Weekly Insights Delivery**: Generate concise, actionable weekly reports delivered via email
- **Privacy Protection**: Ensure no personally identifiable information (PII) is included in any outputs
- **Scalable Architecture**: Provide a foundation for future enhancements and integrations

### Target Audience

- Product managers and growth teams seeking actionable insights from user feedback
- Development teams requiring structured feedback analysis
- Marketing teams interested in monitoring user sentiment and feature adoption

## Technical Architecture

The project employs a modular, layered architecture with clear separation of concerns across three phases:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Phase 1: Core Scraper] --> B[Phase 2: LLM Analytics]
    B --> C[Phase 3: Web Interface]
    
    D[Google Play Store] --> A
    A --> E[SQLite Database]
    E --> B
    B --> F[Groq LLM API]
    B --> G[Email Service]
    G --> H[SMTP Provider]
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style B fill:#f3e5f5
    style C fill:#e8f5e8
```

### Component Breakdown

The system is organized into several key architectural layers:

**Data Layer**: SQLite database with normalized schemas for reviews, themes, and weekly pulses
**Service Layer**: Business logic encapsulated in specialized services for scraping, analysis, and email operations  
**API Layer**: RESTful endpoints exposing functionality to external clients
**Integration Layer**: Third-party service integrations (Google Play, Groq, SMTP)

### Design Patterns

- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through dedicated repositories
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulation in service classes
- **Factory Pattern**: Database connection and configuration management
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven job scheduling for automated pulse generation

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant API
    participant Scraper
    participant DB
    participant LLM
    participant Email
    
    User->>+API: Trigger Review Collection
    API->>+Scraper: Scrape Google Play Reviews
    Scraper->>+DB: Store Filtered Reviews
    Scraper-->>-API: Collection Complete
    API-->>-User: Success Response
    
    User->>+API: Generate Weekly Pulse
    API->>+LLM: Analyze Reviews & Themes
    LLM->>+DB: Retrieve Review Data
    DB-->>-LLM: Review Dataset
    LLM-->>-API: Generated Pulse
    API->>+Email: Send Pulse Email
    Email-->>-User: Delivery Confirmation
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Phase 1 API Server**: `phase-1/src/api/server.ts`
- Express-based REST API with two primary endpoints
- `/api/reviews/scrape` - Initiates review collection from Google Play Store
- `/api/reviews` - Retrieves stored reviews with pagination support

**Phase 2 API Server**: `phase-2/src/api/server.ts`
- Comprehensive API with themed routes for different functional areas
- `/api/themes` - Theme generation and management
- `/api/pulses` - Weekly pulse creation and delivery
- `/api/user-preferences` - User scheduling and preferences

### Core Modules

**Review Scraper**: `phase-1/src/scraper/playstoreScraper.ts`
- Implements token-based pagination for efficient review collection
- Handles rate limiting and error recovery
- Processes up to 50 pages to ensure adequate coverage

**Filter Pipeline**: `phase-1/src/scraper/filters.ts`
- Multi-layered filtering system removing low-quality, duplicate, and privacy-sensitive content
- Regex-based detection for emails, phone numbers, and emojis
- Duplicate detection using normalized text signatures

**Database Layer**: `phase-1/src/db/index.ts`
- SQLite-based schema with optimized indexing for week-based queries
- Transactional operations for bulk data insertion
- Primary key constraints ensuring data integrity

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**: `phase-2/src/config/env.ts`
- Centralized configuration management using environment variables
- Support for multiple deployment environments (development, staging, production)
- Type-safe configuration with sensible defaults

**Database Configuration**: `phase-1/src/config/env.ts`
- SQLite file path configuration with fallback to phase-1 database
- Port configuration for API server binding

### External Dependencies

**Phase 1 Dependencies**:
- `google-play-scraper` - Official Google Play Store review extraction
- `better-sqlite3` - High-performance SQLite database driver
- `express` - Web application framework

**Phase 2 Dependencies**:
- `groq-sdk` - Groq AI model integration for advanced analysis
- `nodemailer` - Email service integration
- `dotenv` - Environment variable management

### Integration Points

**Google Play Store Integration**: Direct API calls to extract public review data
**Groq LLM Integration**: Advanced natural language processing for theme generation and analysis
**Email Service Integration**: SMTP-based email delivery with configurable providers

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Review {
        +string id
        +string platform
        +number rating
        +string title
        +string text
        +string cleanText
        +Date createdAt
        +string weekStart
        +string weekEnd
    }
    
    class ReviewService {
        +scrapeAndStoreReviews()
        +listReviews()
    }
    
    class PlayStoreScraper {
        +scrapeFilteredReviews()
        +processPagination()
    }
    
    class FilterPipeline {
        +passesFilters()
        +basicCleanText()
    }
    
    ReviewService --> PlayStoreScraper
    PlayStoreScraper --> FilterPipeline
    ReviewService --> Review
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Automated Review Collection**: Systematic extraction of Google Play Store reviews with intelligent pagination and filtering
**Intelligent Content Analysis**: Multi-layered filtering removing low-quality, duplicate, and privacy-sensitive content
**Theme Discovery**: AI-powered identification of recurring themes from user feedback
**Weekly Pulse Generation**: Automated creation of concise, actionable weekly insights reports
**Privacy Protection**: Comprehensive PII removal and masking throughout the data pipeline
**Email Delivery**: Automated email scheduling with customizable preferences

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Filtering Pipeline**: `phase-1/src/scraper/filters.ts`
- Multi-criteria filtering including word count thresholds, emoji detection, and PII removal
- Duplicate detection using normalized text signatures
- Configurable filtering parameters for quality control

**Groq Integration**: `phase-2/src/services/groqClient.ts`
- Integration with Groq's high-speed LLM API for rapid theme generation
- Structured prompt engineering for consistent output formatting
- Error handling and retry mechanisms for reliability

**Email Service**: `phase-2/src/services/emailService.ts`
- SMTP-based email delivery with configurable providers
- HTML and plain text email templates
- Recipient validation and preference management

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ReviewCollection
    ReviewCollection --> Filtering --> Storage
    Storage --> ThemeGeneration
    ThemeGeneration --> PulseCreation
    PulseCreation --> EmailDelivery
    EmailDelivery --> [*]
    
    ReviewCollection --> ErrorHandling
    ErrorHandling --> ReviewCollection
```

The system operates as a continuous pipeline, with each phase building upon the previous one to create increasingly sophisticated insights from raw user feedback data.

---

**Sources:**
- [server.ts](phase-1/src/api/server.ts)
- [reviewService.ts](phase-1/src/services/reviewService.ts)
- [playstoreScraper.ts](phase-1/src/scraper/playstoreScraper.ts)
- [filters.ts](phase-1/src/scraper/filters.ts)
- [review.model.ts](phase-1/src/domain/review.model.ts)
- [index.ts](phase-1/src/db/index.ts)
- [server.ts](phase-2/src/api/server.ts)
- [env.ts](phase-2/src/config/env.ts)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [README.md](phase-3/README.md)

</blog>
